message:
fix: complete remaining fixes for Codex token drift (closes #14)

Previous fixes (ca45da4, 141a154) addressed asks #2 and #4. This commit
completes the remaining two asks:

Ask #1 - Doctor now detects unusable tokens via live API probe:
  - probeVaultToken() calls GET /v1/me for Codex profiles to verify the
    access token actually works, catching refresh_token_reused state where
    the JWT looks valid by expiry but the account is actually broken.
  - Profiles with unparseable expiry are now probed instead of silently
    skipped, eliminating the false-pass gap from the original issue.

Ask #3 - Token sync after refresh prevents drift:
  - SyncVaultToIsolatedProfile() copies refreshed vault auth to the
    corresponding isolated profile's codex_home/auth.json after a
    successful token refresh. This prevents the isolated copy from
    retaining a stale (already-consumed) refresh token.
  - syncVaultToIsolated() called from both refreshTool() and
    refreshSingle() in the cmd layer.
  - ErrRefreshTokenReused sentinel + RefreshTokenReusedError type
    replace raw API error dumps with actionable messages that tell
    the user exactly which 'caam login' command to run.
  - IsRefreshTokenReused() detects the error code in API responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
